response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 14+ years of professional experience, this role requires mandatory C++ expertise on Windows with SQL Server/Oracle knowledge and financial domain experience. The resume shows only ~6 months of documented C++ work (ending 2015) with no Windows platform emphasis, no SQL Server/Oracle specialization, and crucially, zero capital markets or fixed-income background. The career has been primarily JavaScript/TypeScript in web and data platforms since 2015, making this a fundamental technical and domain mismatch.
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience (14+ years) with demonstrated expertise in building scalable, low-latency systems across diverse platforms
Strong communication skills evidenced by teaching workshops, open-source project leadership, and mentoring capabilities
Proven ability to rapidly learn and master diverse technology stacks and architectural patterns
concerns: Minimal C++ experience (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015) with no recent hands-on development in the language
No demonstrated expertise in SQL Server, Oracle, or financial domain-specific databases; primary database experience is MongoDB and MySQL
Zero financial services, capital markets, investment banking, or fixed-income product experience; entire recent career trajectory in web and data platforms
